message:
chore(beads): close bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1 board lane summaries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,7 +85,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","title":"Recompose the main detail pane into a modular cockpit: summary, action state, dependencies, metrics, and narrative","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current line-oriented report in the main detail pane with a modular, high-hierarchy layout that makes actionability and context obvious.

Why this matters:
The current detail pane has valuable data, but it is arranged like a narrative dump. Operators should be able to understand state, blockers, risk, and recommended action from a small number of visually distinct modules.

Required scope:
- Create a high-priority summary module that immediately answers: what is this, why does it matter, and what should I do next?
- Separate action state, dependencies, graph signals, and narrative/body content into visually distinct groups.
- Use semantic panels, badges, metric strips, and hierarchy rather than relying on whitespace and colons alone.
- Keep rich long-form fields readable without letting them dominate the top of the pane.

Acceptance:
- The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.
- Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.
- The detail pane feels like a decision cockpit rather than a report body.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. The top modules must answer what this is, why it matters now, and what to do next before the user reads long narrative text.\n2. Make dependency, risk, and action cues scannable enough that the pane helps with prioritization rather than only documentation.\n3. Handle sparse metadata and overlong narrative gracefully so the pane still feels intentional when fields are missing or huge.\n4. Preserve drill links and adjacent-workflow handoffs where they improve operator flow.\n\nFuture-self note: This pane should still be useful when data is messy, incomplete, or verbose. If it only looks good on perfect issues, it is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.\n2. Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.\n3. Sparse, missing, and extremely verbose fields degrade gracefully without destroying hierarchy or readability.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and journey/keyflow coverage prove both normal and degraded detail-pane states, including long content and linked handoffs.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:08:59.032299697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","frankentui","main-view","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.180090767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.562731182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","title":"Overhaul main-screen search, filter, focus, and discoverability so the default workflow feels immediate","description":"Purpose:
Make the main screen fast and legible for both first-time and expert use by upgrading its interaction affordances, not just its visuals.

Required scope:
- Apply the full search-excellence contract to the main screen: search-as-you-type, match counts, stronger active-match emphasis, and better context than a bare query banner.
- Improve filter visibility so active filters, label/repo scopes, and search state are obvious in the shell and screen body.
- Improve focus signaling between list, detail, and any auxiliary controls so users are not forced to infer focus from subtle cursor movement.
- Reduce dependence on long footer prose by surfacing action/discovery cues nearer to the relevant UI regions.

Acceptance:
- Main-view search/filter/focus behavior feels productized rather than bolted on.
- Search results are easier to understand and cycle through than in the current implementation.
- A first-time operator can infer more of the workflow from the interface itself.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Optimize for the first 30 seconds of use: users should understand search, filter, match navigation, and focus ownership from the interface itself.\n2. Treat no-hit and over-filtered states as first-class UX surfaces with clear recovery cues, not as edge leftovers.\n3. Keep expert workflows fast while making novice recovery obvious when focus, filters, or search state become confusing.\n4. Make search/focus behavior deterministic enough that the lower-level and E2E proof beads can assert it precisely.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the main view stops feeling hostile to first-time users. If people can still get lost after filtering or searching, the redesign is incomplete.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Main-view search/filter/focus behavior feels productized rather than bolted on.\n2. Search results, no-hit states, and filter scopes are easy to understand and recover from.\n3. A first-time operator can infer more of the workflow from the interface itself, while expert loops remain fast.\n4. Unit/state, keyflow, and journey proof cover focus recovery, match cycling, filter reset, and no-hit recovery paths with detailed logging.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.753658926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T01:20:37.575083733Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T01:20:37.574599497Z","close_reason":"Completed main-view search/filter/focus discoverability overhaul with deterministic keyflow and journey proof.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","main-view","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.924714042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:00.661715120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:52.292979263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.753658926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:52.694067284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","title":"Rebuild board mode as a real kanban workspace with lane intelligence, richer cards, and interactive layout","description":"Purpose:
Transform board mode from a textual lane report into a genuine kanban-style workspace that rewards switching away from the main screen.

Background:
The audit found that board mode currently behaves more like grouped text plus a single detail card than a serious board. It lacks strong lane summaries, strong spatial memory, and enough visual density to justify itself as a separate view.

Required scope:
- Rebuild lane composition so the operator sees meaningful lane-level signal, not only counts and empty space.
- Improve card presentation, selection, and within-lane navigation so the mode feels spatially and visually distinct.
- Use the new shell/layout/pane primitives so board mode benefits from hit-testing, splitters where appropriate, and strong semantic panels.

Acceptance:
- Board mode feels like a real workflow surface rather than a filtered report.
- Lane-level bottlenecks and WIP pressure are much easier to perceive.
- The mode has a clear visual identity and operational payoff.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat board mode as a true workspace: lane signal, card signal, and board/detail interaction all matter.\n2. Use the new shell, layout, and hit-testing primitives to avoid another one-off mode architecture.\n3. Keep empty-lane handling, grouping, and search/navigation parity while making the surface much more legible.\n\nFuture-self note: Board mode should have a reason to exist. If it still feels like a grouped list with a sidecar report, this bead is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Board mode has a clear lane/card visual identity and stronger lane-level signal than the current implementation.\n2. Selection, focus, search, and detail interaction are productized and easier to understand.\n3. No current board capability is lost while upgrading the presentation.\n4. Unit/keyflow tests cover lane, group, search, and focus behavior, and journey/snapshot evidence proves the rebuilt board across width tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.131362528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.890425776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","frankentui","kanban","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:27.749785129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.131362528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.075086185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.911519314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.460846818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:02.335306678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.833731929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:02.741308355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.244695317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","title":"Add lane summary modules and richer board cards with density tuned for narrow, medium, and wide layouts","description":"Purpose:
Upgrade the visual grammar of the board itself so lanes and cards carry useful information before the detail pane is involved.

Required scope:
- Add lane summary treatments that can surface count, blocked share, stale share, or other lane-health signals rather than only raw totals.
- Rebuild card previews so they communicate status, priority, title, and at least one additional meaningful signal without requiring a detail jump.
- Create width-aware variants so the board can show more useful content instead of large empty gutters.

Acceptance:
- Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity.
- Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines.
- The board stays useful across widths.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Lanes must communicate pressure, health, and comparative meaning at a glance, not just counts.\n2. Cards should preserve spatial memory and still work when titles, labels, or metadata are long, sparse, or numerous.\n3. Empty and nearly empty lanes need intentional treatments so the board still feels informative instead of hollow.\n4. Use width-aware card/lane variants that preserve usefulness under crowding rather than simply shrinking text.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still has large empty gutters or cards that only differ by title text, this bead is underdone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity, including meaningful empty-lane and low-density treatments.\n2. Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines, even with long or messy metadata.\n3. The board stays useful across widths, crowding levels, and sparse/fallback states.\n4. Snapshot and keyflow proof cover lane/card density behavior, empty-lane presentation, and crowded-board layouts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:01.402380525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","density","frankentui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.637445259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.006832223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Snapshot and keyflow proof cover lane/card density behavior, empty-lane presentation, and crowded-board layouts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:20:19.019488079Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T06:20:19.019009293Z","close_reason":"Lane summaries upgraded: health indicator (blocked/clear/empty), type+status icons on cards, blocker/unblocks indicators (⊘N/↓N), priority badges, 8-card preview limit, descriptive empty states.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","density","frankentui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.637445259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.006832223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","title":"Add board hit-testing, pane behavior, and navigation/discoverability parity for a true workspace feel","description":"Purpose:
Make board mode interact like a first-class workspace rather than a passive grouped list.

Required scope:
- Add hit-tested selection and focus movement for lane and card interaction.
- Improve detail split behavior and pane allocation so the operator can actually work from the board instead of just glancing at it.
- Make search, lane jumps, and focus movement more discoverable and less dependent on memorized footer prose.
- Ensure the board uses the shared shell conventions rather than inventing another control language.

Acceptance:
- The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.
- Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation.
- The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Make board interactions fast enough that an operator could plausibly stay in board mode to work, not just glance at it.\n2. Lane jumps, focus changes, and detail split behavior must remain understandable under mouse and keyboard use, including after resizing or filtering.\n3. Treat no-hit, single-lane, and sparse-board states as real operator flows with clear recovery cues.\n4. Keep board-specific discoverability near the relevant lanes/cards rather than outsourcing everything to global help.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still feels like a grouped report after interaction starts, this bead has not landed.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.\n2. Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation, including after resize, filter, and no-hit transitions.\n3. The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic in both dense and sparse states.\n4. Unit/state, hit-region, and journey proof cover lane jumps, split behavior, focus recovery, and board-specific recovery flows with verbose diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:07.994392240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","discoverability","frankentui","interaction","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.385950129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.779058570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.171426497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","title":"Rebuild graph mode as a topology exploration surface instead of a node profile report","description":"Purpose:
Turn graph mode into a mode where users actually understand structure, bottlenecks, and neighborhoods instead of reading a long metric profile for one node at a time.

Background:
Current graph mode is analytically useful, but experientially it is still a ranked list plus an ASCII ego box and a long metric section. That is not enough to create graph intuition or make the mode feel indispensable.

Required scope:
- Reframe graph mode around neighborhood exploration and relationship navigation.
- Improve the presentation of graph metrics so they aid interpretation without dominating the screen.
- Add stronger cross-links and drill paths so graph mode can hand off effectively to detail/history/other views.

Acceptance:
- Users can perceive graph structure faster and more confidently than in the current implementation.
- The mode feels graph-native rather than report-native.
- Metric presentation supports topology understanding instead of burying it.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Graph mode should prioritize topology comprehension and traversal confidence over static metric dumping.\n2. The rebuilt graph should use badges, panels, and links that support action, not just observation.\n3. Preserve the analytically valuable metrics, but present them in a way that earns their screen real estate.\n\nFuture-self note: This mode is where  proves it is more than a task list. If the operator cannot feel graph structure quickly, the redesign missed the point.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Graph mode materially improves neighborhood comprehension and traversal.\n2. Metric presentation is denser and more actionable than the current long report format.\n3. Cross-links to adjacent workflows exist where they improve operator flow.\n4. Deterministic tests and journey/snapshot proof cover topology-focused interactions and width-tier behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.206885414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.903466094Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","topology","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:28.065882333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.206885414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.586153120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:03.180462475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.950941730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:03.594684496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:57.343846863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:04.015781545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:57.775337062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","title":"Redesign graph neighborhood visualization and relationship traversal around blockers, dependents, cycles, and focal context","description":"Purpose:
Give graph mode a stronger neighborhood metaphor so the operator can reason about topology, not just metrics.

Required scope:
- Rework the focal-node presentation so blockers, dependents, and local structure are easier to parse than the current mostly textual composition.
- Improve traversal affordances for moving through relationships and local neighborhoods.
- Surface cycle membership and local criticality in a way that helps graph understanding instead of hiding it in later text sections.

Acceptance:
- Neighborhood structure is materially easier to understand than in the current graph screen.
- Relationship traversal feels intentional and supports exploration.
- The focal graph area earns the name "graph mode".","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Prioritize topology comprehension over ornament: blockers, dependents, cycles, and focal context should be faster to read than in the current graph.\n2. Traversal must remain usable under dense local neighborhoods, sparse neighborhoods, and cycle-heavy cases.\n3. The focal presentation should make it clear what changed as the user traverses, so exploration does not become disorienting.\n4. Responsive fallbacks must preserve relationship meaning even when there is not enough room for the richest composition.\n\nFuture-self note: If graph exploration still feels mentally expensive or easy to get lost in, this bead is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Neighborhood structure is materially easier to understand than in the current graph screen.\n2. Relationship traversal feels intentional and supports exploration across dense, sparse, and cycle-heavy local neighborhoods.\n3. Responsive and degraded layouts still preserve the core relationship story rather than collapsing into ambiguous text.\n4. Unit/state, snapshot, and journey proof cover traversal, focal-context updates, and dense-neighborhood edge cases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.566060523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:16.529383305Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","layout","relationships","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:58.167971592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:58.566932927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.566060523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
